source.name: German Federal Foreign Office — Visa for study purposes

extraSources.name: Make it in Germany — Student visa

requirements.label: University admission or conditional admission letter
requirements.detail: A confirmed (or conditional) admission letter from a recognised German higher-education institution, or proof of a Studienkolleg place.

requirements.label: Proof of financial resources (blocked account)
requirements.detail: Evidence you can cover living costs — most applicants open a blocked account holding the annual minimum and releasing a fixed sum each month.

requirements.label: Health insurance valid in Germany
requirements.detail: Travel/incoming health insurance covering the period until enrolment, then statutory or recognised private student health insurance.

requirements.label: Proof of language proficiency
requirements.detail: English-taught programmes: IELTS/TOEFL/Duolingo/PTE as the university specifies. German-taught: TestDaF/DSH/Goethe.

requirements.label: Valid passport
requirements.detail: Passport valid for the intended stay, plus biometric photos meeting embassy specification.

requirements.label: APS certificate (nationality-dependent)
requirements.detail: Applicants from certain countries (e.g. India, Vietnam, China, Pakistan) must obtain an APS certificate before applying.

requirements.label: Tuition / proof-of-funds transfer
requirements.detail: If transferring tuition or topping up a blocked account from abroad, use a low-fee provider to avoid losing money on FX.

requirements.label: Connectivity on arrival
requirements.detail: An eSIM gives you a working number and data the moment you land — useful for city registration (Anmeldung) and bank setup.

requirements.label: Proof of accommodation (often requested)
requirements.detail: Many consulates ask for a tenancy agreement or dorm confirmation; book early as student housing is scarce.
